_comment: CANONICAL authored brochure copy for curaos.example.com. PUBLIC product brochure (no PHI, no tenant data). This workspace mirror is the canonical authoring source per the repo-boundary rule; scripts/build.sh on the box resolves --content-dir to the repo copy (curaos/curaos-website/content/site.json), which is kept byte-synced with this file. examples/site-content/site.json is a MINIMAL build/test fixture only. Copy here is the OpenDesign Marketing Redesign (project curaos-marketing) reworded to current live reality and folded into the renderer schema: every renderer-consumed field (siteName, tagline, eyebrow, headline, subhead, positioning, description, pillars[], architecture, deployProfiles[], status, quickstart, footer) is enriched; extra authoring keys (stats, apps, capabilities, demoLinks, getStarted) are retained for the doc graph and future renderer growth even though the current renderer ignores them. Every claim is verified against the CuraOS charter (AGENTS.md) and what is actually live: 22 frontend app projects, 20 web apps in the local render gate, 19 public vanity web hosts, and 38 routed backend services exposing 83 generated gateway domains. Capability claims that are not yet GA are framed as design intent, not shipped. Zero em-dashes or en-dashes (binding rule). App and demo hrefs are literal https URLs (the live hosts); docs/releases/demo use {docsUrl}/{releasesUrl}/{demoUrl} tokens resolved from the same build flags as the hero CTAs.
headline: The care platform you actually own
subhead: A composable, self-hosted-first stack: a generic neutral core with opt-in overlays for health, education, and ERP. Twenty-two frontend app projects and 38 routed backend services run right now, self-hosted on Kubernetes, composed from one codebase and one shared design system. It deploys to cloud, on-prem, hybrid, or fully air-gapped.
positioning: Self-hosted first. Event-led. Multi-tenant from one codebase. No managed-cloud lock-in.
description: CuraOS is a composable, self-hosted-first platform: a generic neutral core with opt-in vertical overlays (Health, Education, ERP). It runs today as 22 frontend app projects on a shared design system and 38 routed backend services on self-hosted Kubernetes, with single sign-on through Pocket-ID, and is designed to deploy across cloud, on-prem, hybrid, and air-gapped targets. CuraOS is in active development (pre-1.0); the deployment profiles and overlay depth harden along the roadmap.

pillars.title: Self-hosted first
pillars.blurb: You run it on your own infrastructure, inside your own network boundary. Managed cloud is an option, never a requirement, and there is no lock-in to leave behind.

pillars.title: Generic before vertical
pillars.blurb: The core stays domain-neutral and reusable. Health, education, and ERP arrive as overlays that depend on the core through documented seams; they never fork it.

pillars.title: Event-led
pillars.blurb: Services talk over durable, versioned events first and sync APIs second. State changes stay observable, replayable, and auditable, so the system stays loosely coupled by design.

pillars.title: Multi-tenant
pillars.blurb: Tenant isolation is a core boundary, not a setting. PHI and PII live in overlay schemas scoped per tenant and never bleed into the neutral core. SaaS, on-prem, and hybrid all run from one codebase.

pillars.title: Builder-led
pillars.blurb: A workflow and BPM engine plus an app and site builder. Operators compose flows and surfaces against documented seams instead of forking the platform.

pillars.title: Composable
pillars.blurb: Services, libraries, and clients ship independently and combine per tenant and per market. Opt into the verticals you need; each overlay is additive on a shared foundation, not a separate fork.

apps.caption: Twenty-two app projects, one design system
apps.intro: The suite tracks 22 frontend app projects: 20 web apps plus 2 Expo mobile apps. Nineteen web apps are reachable today on public vanity hosts; Builder Studio is a cluster web app without a public vanity host. Sign-in is OIDC through Pocket-ID, brokered by the identity service into a CuraOS session. Dark mode and right-to-left Arabic are built in and persist across reloads.

capabilities.caption: The platform underneath
capabilities.intro: Every app sits on the same foundation. The neutral core provides reusable capabilities; vertical overlays extend it. Behavior is built, not forked. The current local reference stack routes 38 backend services through the gateway and exposes 83 generated `/api/v1` domains.

items.title: Workflow and BPM core
items.blurb: Orchestrates human tasks, automation, and SLA timing. Every domain routes through it, so process logic lives in one place.

items.title: App and site builder
items.blurb: Generates admin, ops, and external surfaces from BPM definitions, domain contracts, and shared theming.

items.title: Automation core
items.blurb: Low-code actions, connectors, and scheduling so integrations and routine work are configured rather than coded.

items.title: Neutral capabilities
items.blurb: Identity, tenancy, party, audit, notify, search, reports, storage, calendar, tasks, documents, commerce, and more, all vertical-agnostic.

items.title: Vertical overlays
items.blurb: HealthStack (patient, encounter, clinical, consent, interop) plus Education and ERP, extending the core through documented seams.

items.title: Versioned contracts
items.blurb: APIs and durable events carry versioned schemas with deprecation windows, so services evolve without breaking consumers.

architecture.caption: Dependency points downward, always. Every overlay builds on the neutral core; the core never reaches up into a vertical. That invariant is what keeps tenant data boundaried and lets a deployment run health, education, and ERP side by side, or none of them, without forking. Underneath, CuraOS runs as composable services on Kubernetes with CNPG Postgres, Valkey, and event-led messaging; the public edge is fronted by Caddy and Cloudflare.

deployProfiles.name: Cloud SaaS
deployProfiles.blurb: Hosted multi-tenant for teams that want managed convenience. The same binaries you can pull on-prem the day you want out, with per-tenant isolation and horizontal scale.

deployProfiles.name: On-prem
deployProfiles.blurb: Runs entirely inside your data center or VPC on self-hosted Kubernetes, with overlays opt-in: your identity provider, your network policy, your backup story. This is how the live deployment runs today.

deployProfiles.name: Hybrid
deployProfiles.blurb: Keep regulated workloads inside your boundary while bursting non-sensitive services out. The vendor runs the control plane; your data plane, audit, and secrets stay on your infrastructure, and the event bus spans both sides.

deployProfiles.name: Home lab / air-gap
deployProfiles.blurb: Zero-egress and fully offline, designed for zero external calls: no phone-home, no CDN. Zero-egress is already proven for the platform's own infrastructure and this very site, not yet for a full product tenant.

status.caption: What is real today, what is on the roadmap
status.intro: CuraOS is in active development (pre-1.0). We label shipped capability and planned capability separately, on purpose. Nothing below is dressed up as more finished than it is, and we would rather under-claim. Scaffolded means the service exists and runs, not that every feature is complete. Counts reflect current buildout and will change.

columns.heading: Real today
items: 22 frontend app projects on one design system: 20 web apps plus 2 Expo mobile apps
19 public vanity web hosts and 20 local web apps exercised by the real-data render gate
38 routed backend services exposing 83 generated gateway domains
OIDC sign-in (Authorization Code with PKCE) through Pocket-ID, brokered by the identity service into a CuraOS session
Workflow and BPM core, app and site builder, and automation core in place
Neutral core plus overlay model with the downward dependency invariant holding, and PHI/PII scoped to overlay schemas
Air-gap zero-egress proven for the platform's own infrastructure and this site

columns.heading: On the roadmap
items: 1.0 stabilization: frozen APIs, upgrade guarantees, and semantic versioning
Full domain depth inside each service, from scaffold to feature-complete
A shipped curaos installer and CLI; the quickstart below is illustrative, not yet released
A public, self-serve multi-tenant demo tenant and hosted sandbox
GA hardening of the cloud, hybrid, and air-gap deployment profiles, with air-gap zero-egress proven end to end for a full product tenant
Education and ERP overlays brought to parity with HealthStack
Compliance attestations packaged per deployment profile

demoLinks.caption: See it running
demoLinks.intro: These are live, reachable surfaces, not screenshots. Sign-in is OIDC through Pocket-ID; some apps require an account. The routed backend stack exposes 83 generated gateway domains behind `https://api.example.com/api/v1`.

getStarted.caption: Run it on your own infrastructure
getStarted.body: CuraOS deploys to Kubernetes with CNPG Postgres, Valkey, and an ingress controller. The full self-host walkthrough, including OIDC setup and the service catalog, lives in the docs. The commands below are illustrative of the target flow.
lines: ok: neutral core up; add overlays per tenant

footer._comment: Deploy-variable hrefs use {docsUrl}/{demoUrl}/{releasesUrl} tokens resolved from the same build flags as the hero CTAs, so header and footer share one source of truth. Static links (GitHub org, live apps) stay literal.
footer.note: CuraOS is independent software you self-host: a composable, self-hosted-first care platform, in active development (pre-1.0). No managed-cloud lock-in.
